title: Dual-Resistor Tuning
learningObjectives: Adjust two series resistors to hit a target total resistance
Relate resistance sum to current draw
Preserve safe current while changing voltage
introText: Stacking resistors lets you set precise totals. You'll balance two resistors to reach a target resistance while keeping current at a safe level.

steps.instructionText: Add a 12V source and two resistors in series, wired into a single loop.
steps.feedbackOnSuccess: Loop complete with two resistors ready for tuning.
steps.feedbackOnFailure: Ensure both resistors and the source are wired together with no open ends.

steps.instructionText: Adjust the pair to reach at least 2.4 kΩ of total resistance.
steps.feedbackOnSuccess: Total resistance hits the 2.4 kΩ target.
steps.feedbackOnFailure: Increase one or both resistors until the total reaches 2400Ω or higher.

steps.instructionText: Verify the current is below 6 mA at 12V.
steps.feedbackOnSuccess: Current is under 6 mA — safe for delicate loads.
steps.feedbackOnFailure: Increase total resistance slightly or lower the source voltage to reduce current.

questions.questionText: How do two resistors in series combine?
questions.explanation: Series resistors add directly: R_total = R1 + R2.

questions.questionText: Why does higher total resistance lower current?
options: Ohm's Law: higher R reduces I for the same V
questions.explanation: From I = V/R, increasing R decreases I when V is fixed.
